title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Rheumatoid Arthritis
intro: Herbal teas for Rheumatoid arthritis are a type of natural remedy that utilizes the medicinal properties of various herbs to alleviate the symptoms associated with this chronic autoimmune disorder. These teas can be effective in treating rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce joint pain and swelling, as well as slow down the progression of the disease. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat rheumatoid arthritis include ginger tea, which reduces inflammation and pain; turmeric tea, which contains curcumin that has potent anti-inflammatory properties; peppermint tea, which helps to relax muscles and reduce pain; chamomile tea, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce joint pain and swelling; and willow bark tea, which contains salicylic acid that helps to reduce inflammation and pain. Additionally, other herbal teas such as rosehip tea, dandelion tea, and licorice root tea may also be beneficial in treating rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas, derived from the rhizomes of turmeric plants, have been extensively studied for their potential in treating rheumatoid arthritis (RA) due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents, primarily curcuminoids, specifically curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, have been found to suppress pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thus providing relief from joint pain and inflammation associated with RA. By modulating the immune response and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, Curcuma longa teas help to reduce the severity of RA symptoms, improve joint function, and enhance overall quality of life. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas for RA treatment include reduced reliance on pharmaceutical medications, lower risk of side effects, and improved management of chronic inflammation, making it a promising adjunctive therapy for patients with RA.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of water, 1 teaspoon of dried turmeric root, 1 tablespoon of honey (optional)
Heat the water in a pot until it boils.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried turmeric root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and add honey if desired. Drink 2-3 cups a day to help manage rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Molecular biology reports", Curcuma longa teas for rheumatoid arthritis may benefit patients by up-regulating antioxidant potential, inhibiting cartilage degradation, down-regulating inflammatory cytokines, and suppressing oxidative stress.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be beneficial in treating rheumatoid arthritis, but they may cause side effects such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach ulcers in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To use Curcuma longa teas safely, it is recommended to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming them on an empty stomach or with other medications that may interact with their active compounds.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Curcuma longa tea for rheumatoid arthritis treatment.
Start with 1/2 cup of tea, 1-2 times a day, for 1 week.
Monitor symptoms and adjust dosage to 1 cup, 2-3 times a day, if needed.
Maintain consistent dosage and frequency for 2-3 months, then reassess.
Combine Curcuma longa tea with other treatments, as directed by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger teas, have been traditionally used to treat rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce joint pain and swelling. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby alleviating the symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of ginger tea help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which are key contributors to the development and progression of rheumatoid arthritis. By consuming ginger tea regularly, individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may experience relief from joint pain, stiffness, and swelling, as well as improved overall health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of fresh Zingiber officinale (ginger) root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a cup.
Peel the ginger root with a spoon and chop it into small pieces.
Add the chopped ginger to the cup and pour in the boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink 1-2 cups of the ginger tea, 2-3 times a day, to help manage rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Molecular biology reports", Zingiber officinale teas for rheumatoid arthritis may have benefits such as down-regulating inflammatory cytokines and suppressing oxidative stress.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to potentially alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis, but some individuals may experience side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its active compound gingerol. To minimize potential interactions and adverse effects, it is recommended to consume Zingiber officinale teas in moderation, especially when combined with other medications, and to avoid taking it before surgery or on an empty stomach.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional before using Zingiber officinale tea.
Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 250-500 mg of Zingiber officinale extract per cup.
Gradually increase dosage to 1 tablespoon (15-20 mg) per cup.
Continue treatment for 2-3 months to assess effectiveness and adjust dosage.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the roots of the licorice plant, have been used to treat rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of glycyrrhiza glabra, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to the development of rheumatoid arthritis. By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, glycyrrhiza glabra teas may help to alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis, such as joint pain and swelling. The benefits of glycyrrhiza glabra teas in treating rheumatoid arthritis include reduced inflammation, improved joint mobility, and enhanced overall quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root and 1 cup of water in a pot.
Heat the water over low heat and add the dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root.
Simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid and discard the root.
Let the tea cool down and add honey to taste, if desired.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Inflammopharmacology", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for rheumatoid arthritis may be beneficial as they contain liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin which can reduce the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and alleviate symptoms in a collagen-induced arthritis model in rats.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating rheumatoid arthritis, but it may cause side effects such as water retention, high blood pressure, and potassium deficiency due to its licorice root content. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas safely, take them in moderation, limit the duration of treatment, and monitor your body's response, as prolonged consumption can exacerbate existing health conditions like kidney disease or heart problems.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor to confirm Glycyrrhiza glabra tea usage for rheumatoid arthritis.
Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root per cup of boiling water.
Continue using Glycyrrhiza glabra tea for at least 3-6 months to see benefits.
Monitor your symptoms and adjust tea usage as directed by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to help alleviate the symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis, a chronic inflammatory disease. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginkgo biloba, particularly its flavonoids and terpenoids, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making it easier to manage the condition. The bioactive constituents of ginkgo biloba, including bilobalide and ginkgolides, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and cytokines, which contribute to the development of rheumatoid arthritis. The regular consumption of ginkgo biloba teas may also help to improve joint mobility and reduce the severity of symptoms associated with rheumatoid arthritis, providing relief and improving overall quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to sweeten the tea.
Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day, preferably on an empty stomach.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Presse medicale (Paris, France : 1983)", Ginkgo biloba teas for rheumatoid arthritis were found to provide significantly greater pain relief and walking tolerance compared to a placebo after 6 months of treatment, and this improvement continued throughout the whole duration of the study.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to potentially alleviate rheumatoid arthritis symptoms, but its consumption may lead to side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. To safely use Ginkgo biloba teas for this purpose, it is essential to consume them in moderation, avoid taking them with other medications, and monitor your body's response to prevent adverse interactions and reactions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup Ginkgo biloba tea 2 times a day
Start with 250-500mg Ginkgo biloba extract per cup
Gradually increase to 1-2 cups per day as needed
Continue using Ginkgo biloba tea for 8-12 weeks
Consult doctor before combining with other medications

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polysaccharides, help to modulate the immune system and reduce inflammation, thus alleviating the symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis. By reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and increasing the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, Echinacea purpurea teas help to treat rheumatoid arthritis by reducing joint pain, swelling, and stiffness. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat rheumatoid arthritis include reduced reliance on pharmaceutical medications, improved quality of life, and a decrease in the risk of developing chronic diseases associated with long-term use of conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Combine the flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "BioMed research international", Echinacea purpurea teas may be beneficial for rheumatoid arthritis due to its immunostimulating activity, which is attributed to its polyphenols, terpenoids, and alkaloids, as well as its ability to induce cytokines and phagocyte cells and inhibit iNOS, PGE, and COX-2 synthesis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to potentially alleviate some symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and diarrhea, and interactions with blood-thinning medications. To minimize risks when using Echinacea purpurea teas, individuals should be cautious with large doses, as they may exacerbate existing conditions or interact with other medications, and consider avoiding it if they have sensitive stomachs or allergies.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 2 times a day.
Start with 1/2 cup for first week and increase to 1 cup.
Take 1/2 cup 30 minutes before meals for better absorption.
Use 2-3 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers per cup.
Drink tea for 8-12 weeks to see noticeable improvements.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been studied for their potential therapeutic benefits in treating rheumatoid arthritis, a chronic inflammatory condition characterized by joint pain and swelling. The anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, thereby providing relief from the symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to have a positive effect on reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas has been associated with reduced symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis, improved joint mobility, and enhanced overall quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it starts boiling.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1 cup, 2-3 times a day for relief from rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating rheumatoid arthritis, but using it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To safely use Lavandula angustifolia teas, it's essential to start with a low dose, gradually increasing the amount as needed, and to avoid consuming it in large quantities, especially before bedtime, as it may exacerbate insomnia and disrupt sleep patterns.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional before using Lavandula angustifolia tea for rheumatoid arthritis.
Drink 1-2 cups of Lavandula angustifolia tea 2-3 times a day for relief.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers in each cup of boiling water.
Use Lavandula angustifolia tea for 4-6 weeks to assess its effectiveness on rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
Combine Lavandula angustifolia tea with other arthritis treatments under a healthcare professional's guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Rheum palmatum teas have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Rheum palmatum, such as emodin and rhein, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with rheumatoid arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and inducing apoptosis in immune cells. Regular consumption of Rheum palmatum teas may also help to improve joint function, reduce stiffness, and promote overall well-being in individuals suffering from this debilitating condition. The benefits of using Rheum palmatum teas to treat rheumatoid arthritis include reduced reliance on pharmaceutical medications, improved quality of life, and potential long-term management of symptoms.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Rheum palmatum leaves. Clean them with water to remove dirt.
Bring 4 cups of water to a boil in a large pot. Add 2 cups of the cleaned Rheum palmatum leaves to the pot.
Reduce heat to medium and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes. This helps release the herb's properties into the water.
Strain the liquid into a separate container using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve. Discard the Rheum palmatum leaves.
Let the tea cool down. Drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day. You can also store the tea in the fridge for later use.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rheum palmatum teas can be used as a potential treatment for rheumatoid arthritis, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners and diabetes medications. To use Rheum palmatum teas safely, take precautions by monitoring your blood pressure and sugar levels, as it may lower them, and avoid consuming it in large quantities or for extended periods to minimize the risk of kidney damage and other adverse effects.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Rheum palmatum tea for rheumatoid arthritis.
Drink 1 cup of Rheum palmatum tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Rheum palmatum per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup if needed.
Use Rheum palmatum tea for 2-3 months to see noticeable improvements.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents, such as sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, in yarrow tea help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with rheumatoid arthritis. By consuming yarrow tea, individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may experience relief from symptoms, including reduced joint pain and stiffness, and improved overall quality of life. The benefits of yarrow tea in treating rheumatoid arthritis include its ability to modulate the immune system, reduce oxidative stress, and promote healing and repair of damaged tissues.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Achillea millefolium (yarrow) flowers.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the yarrow flowers in a cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it to remove the flowers.
Add honey to taste, if needed. The recommended dose is 1-2 cups of tea per day.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can help alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, and allergic reactions, including skin rashes and itching. Precautions to take when using Achillea millefolium teas include starting with small doses to monitor individual tolerance, avoiding use during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and being cautious when combining with other medications that slow blood clotting, as it may increase the risk of bleeding.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Achillea millefolium tea for rheumatoid arthritis treatment.
Start with 1 cup of tea, 2-3 times a day, for 7-10 days to assess tolerance.
Gradually increase to 2 cups of tea, 2-3 times a day, for long-term management.
Monitor and adjust dosages as advised by your doctor or based on individual response.
Combine with other treatments and therapies as recommended by your healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Withania somnifera teas, also known as ashwagandha tea, have been traditionally used to treat rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of ashwagandha, such as withanolides and alkaloids, help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, thereby alleviating the symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis. Regular consumption of ashwagandha tea has been shown to improve joint health, reduce pain and swelling, and enhance overall quality of life for individuals suffering from this ailment. The benefits of using ashwagandha tea to treat rheumatoid arthritis include reduced reliance on conventional medications, improved immune function, and a decrease in the risk of long-term complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Withania somnifera root and 2 cups of water.
Boil the water in a pot and add 1 tablespoon of dried Withania somnifera root.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the root. Add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, to help manage rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Molecular biology reports", Withania somnifera teas for rheumatoid arthritis may have potential benefits such as up-regulating antioxidant potential, inhibiting cartilage degradation, down-regulating inflammatory cytokines, and suppressing oxidative stress.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Withania somnifera teas can be used to treat rheumatoid arthritis, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions and interactions with other medications. When using Withania somnifera teas to treat rheumatoid arthritis, take precautions by monitoring your body's response and adjusting the dosage accordingly, and also consider the potential impact of long-term use on liver and kidney function.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Withania somnifera teas for rheumatoid arthritis.
Drink 2 tablespoons of Withania somnifera tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 1 cup of warm water after drinking the tea for hydration.
Combine Withania somnifera tea with other arthritis treatments as advised by doctor.
Monitor your symptoms and adjust dosage (1-2 tablespoons) as needed with doctor's guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herbal preparation helps to alleviate the symptoms of this ailment by reducing pain, inflammation, and swelling, thereby improving the quality of life for patients. The bioactive constituents, including quinine, quinidine, and other alkaloids, contribute to its therapeutic effects, which help to modulate the immune response and reduce oxidative stress. By incorporating Cinchona officinalis teas into a treatment plan, individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may experience relief from symptoms and improved overall health, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for managing this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Cinchona bark in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally on an empty stomach, for relief from rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be effective in treating rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory properties, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and stomach upset, as well as headaches and dizziness in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it's essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase the amount, avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, and be cautious when combining it with other medications or supplements.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Cinchona officinalis for rheumatoid arthritis.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Cinchona officinalis in 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain the liquid.
Drink 2-3 cups a day, 3 times a week for 2-3 weeks.
Monitor your symptoms and adjust the dosage as advised by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to treat rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help to reduce joint pain and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng, including ginsenosides, have been shown to modulate the immune system and inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby alleviating the symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of Panax ginseng teas may help to reduce oxidative stress and improve overall health, making it a potential adjunctive treatment for rheumatoid arthritis. The benefits of using Panax ginseng teas to treat rheumatoid arthritis include reduced joint pain, improved joint function, and a decreased risk of disease progression, making it a promising natural remedy for this chronic condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Panax ginseng root, a tea infuser or a coffee filter, and 1 cup of boiling water.
Put the dried Panax ginseng root in the tea infuser or the coffee filter.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the ginseng root in the infuser or filter.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then carefully remove the infuser or filter.
Drink the tea warm, 1-2 times a day, after consulting with a healthcare professional for the best results.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "International Immunopharmacology", Panax ginseng teas, specifically Panaxynol (a minor component of Panax ginseng), may be beneficial for rheumatoid arthritis as it has been found to promote apoptosis, inhibit proliferation and migration of fibroblast-like synovial cells, and reduce joint inflammation in animal models, potentially alleviating RA symptoms through the TLR4/NF-κB signaling pathway.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can help alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis, but possible side effects may include insomnia, headaches, and stomach upset in some individuals. When using Panax ginseng teas to treat this ailment, it's essential to take precautions, such as starting with low doses and gradually increasing as needed, to minimize the risk of adverse reactions, including interactions with medications and exacerbation of high blood pressure or bleeding disorders.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Panax ginseng tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of ginseng per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons per cup after 1 week.
Use 8-12 ounces of tea per serving for at least 6-8 weeks.
Monitor symptoms and consult a doctor if no improvement after 3 months.

By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, Rosmarinus officinalis teas help to alleviate symptoms such as joint pain and swelling, improving the quality of life for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. The benefits of using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat rheumatoid arthritis include reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a promising natural remedy for this chronic condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.

preparations.herb_desc: Angelica sinensis teas, also known as dong quai, have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Angelica sinensis, including ferulic acid, caffeic acid, and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which contribute to the development of rheumatoid arthritis. These herbal teas may help to treat rheumatoid arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, as well as promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines and antioxidants. Regular consumption of Angelica sinensis teas has been reported to provide relief from rheumatoid arthritis symptoms, including pain, stiffness, and swelling, making it a potential natural remedy for managing this chronic condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Angelica sinensis root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried Angelica sinensis root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the liquid, if desired, to improve taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology, there is no specific information about Angelica sinensis teas for rheumatoid arthritis in the provided study, but it does mention Angelica as one of the herbs associated with significant adverse effects.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Angelica sinensis teas can help alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis, but potential side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, diarrhea, and abdominal pain due to its potential to interact with certain medications. To minimize risks, it's recommended to start with low doses and monitor your body's response, as well as to avoid taking Angelica sinensis teas during pregnancy and breastfeeding due to a lack of comprehensive safety data.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as nettle tea, have been traditionally used to treat rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby minimizing joint pain and swelling. The bioactive constituents present in Urtica dioica, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory activities. Regular consumption of Urtica dioica teas has been reported to provide relief from rheumatoid arthritis symptoms, improve quality of life, and reduce the need for conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves. You can grow them in your garden or buy from a store.
Wash the leaves with water to remove dirt and impurities.
Chop 2 tablespoons of the leaves into small pieces. You can use a knife or scissors.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the chopped leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Planta medica, Urtica dioica teas for rheumatoid arthritis may be beneficial as part of a special formulation (MA212) that significantly improved WOMAC pain scores by 29.87 in patients with gonarthritis, demonstrating a significant superiority over placebo.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosa rugosa teas have been traditionally used to treat rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce joint pain and swelling. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes that contribute to the progression of rheumatoid arthritis. Rosa rugosa teas have been found to help treat this ailment by reducing oxidative stress, improving joint mobility, and promoting overall well-being. The benefits of using Rosa rugosa teas to treat rheumatoid arthritis include reduced reliance on pharmaceutical medications, improved quality of life, and a natural approach to managing chronic pain and inflammation.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Rosa rugosa flowers and leaves.
Combine the flowers and leaves in a large bowl and cover with 4 cups of boiling water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Discard the solids and add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea. Stir well.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally 30 minutes before meals, to help alleviate rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Rheum officinale teas have been traditionally used to treat rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help to reduce joint pain and swelling. The bioactive constituents present in Rheum officinale, such as rhein and emodin, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that contribute to its therapeutic effects. These constituents help to modulate the immune response, reduce pro-inflammatory cytokines, and prevent the progression of joint damage, thereby alleviating the symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis. The benefits of using Rheum officinale teas to treat this ailment include reduced joint pain and swelling, improved mobility, and a decrease in the reliance on non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs).
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh or dried Rheum officinale roots. Clean them with water.
Cut the roots into small pieces. You need 2 tablespoons of the cut roots.
Put the cut roots in a pot and add 4 cups of water. Bring the water to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Strain the liquid.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, after meals. Store the leftover tea in the fridge.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rheum officinale teas can be beneficial in treating rheumatoid arthritis, but its use may also lead to side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high content of anthraquinones, which can be irritating to the digestive system. To minimize potential harm, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, and refrain from using it for extended periods, as prolonged use may lead to dependence and other complications.

preparations.herb_desc: Tanacetum parthenium teas, also known as feverfew, have been used traditionally to treat various health conditions, including rheumatoid arthritis. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce joint pain and inflammation associated with rheumatoid arthritis. The bioactive constituents, such as parthenolide and sesquiterpene lactones, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and pain. Regular consumption of Tanacetum parthenium teas has been found to provide relief from rheumatoid arthritis symptoms, including joint pain, stiffness, and swelling.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Tanacetum parthenium leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the gathered Tanacetum parthenium leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day, after consulting a doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat rheumatoid arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. By consuming Salvia officinalis teas, individuals may experience relief from joint pain and swelling, as well as improved overall health. The benefits of Salvia officinalis teas in treating rheumatoid arthritis include reduced reliance on conventional medications and a natural approach to managing chronic inflammation.

Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dried leaves in the infuser or strainer.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of Salvia officinalis tea 2-3 times a day, as needed for relief from rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia miltiorrhiza teas, also known as Danshen tea, have been traditionally used to treat rheumatoid arthritis due to its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The tea's bioactive constituents, including salvianolic acid, lithospermic acid, and tanshinones, help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, alleviating the symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis. By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, Salvia miltiorrhiza teas help to reduce joint pain and swelling, improving the quality of life for individuals with this condition. The benefits of using Salvia miltiorrhiza teas to treat rheumatoid arthritis include reduced reliance on pharmaceutical medications, improved immune function, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Salvia miltiorrhiza root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried root in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally on an empty stomach, for 1-2 months to see the benefits for rheumatoid arthritis.

To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to consume Salvia miltiorrhiza teas in moderation and monitor blood pressure, as excessive consumption may lead to cardiovascular issues and interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners.
